# Refactor Readiness Checklist: Dataset Curation Refactor

**Purpose**: Validate that the requirements and plan are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for implementation of the dataset curation refactor.
**Created**: 2026-05-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are partition selection requirements defined for crop generation, crop browsing, and utility operations? [Completeness, Spec FR-001..FR-006]
- [X] CHK002 Are requirements documented for the final partition containing fewer than 10,000 images? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK003 Are crop browsing requirements complete for filtering by partition, class label, crop identity, and source image? [Completeness, Spec FR-006]
- [X] CHK004 Are requirements defined for all integrated utility workflows: missing-crop detection, external moved-crop import, label standardization, crop refresh, and save workflows? [Completeness, Spec FR-009]
- [X] CHK005 Are requirements documented for preserving original image files during every curation operation? [Completeness, Spec FR-018]
- [X] CHK006 Are requirements defined for recoverable annotation updates, including changed-file visibility and reviewer review before large operations? [Completeness, Spec FR-019]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK007 Is "stable manifest identity" defined clearly enough to distinguish it from crop filename, class folder, and shape index? [Clarity, Spec FR-016; Data Model CropRecord]
- [X] CHK008 Is "refresh changed crops" specified with enough criteria to determine which images are affected? [Clarity, Spec FR-005; Success Criteria SC-007]
- [X] CHK009 Is the partition ordering rule unambiguous for future batches and repeated sessions? [Clarity, Spec Assumptions; Plan Technical Context]
- [X] CHK010 Is "explicit import/apply" for external moved-crop changes defined clearly enough to prevent automatic trust of folder moves? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications; Contract Import External Moved Crops]
- [X] CHK011 Is "soft-delete" defined with clear pending, cancelled, confirmed, and saved states? [Clarity, Spec Clarifications; Data Model PendingChange]
- [X] CHK012 Are reviewable summaries specified with the required contents: processed items, changed annotations, skipped items, warnings, errors, and changed files? [Clarity, Spec FR-017; Contract Common Result Shape]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK013 Do the spec, data model, and operation contract consistently treat crops as derived artifacts rather than source truth? [Consistency, Spec Assumptions; Plan Phase 1; Data Model CropManifest]
- [X] CHK014 Do label requirements consistently require approved class labels with spaces and not underscores across annotations, display, and crop grouping? [Consistency, Spec FR-012..FR-014; Data Model ClassLabelVocabulary]
- [X] CHK015 Do GUI-primary correction requirements align with the external moved-crop import contract as a legacy/manual workflow? [Consistency, Spec Clarifications; Contract Import External Moved Crops]
- [X] CHK016 Do deletion requirements align across user scenarios, functional requirements, pending changes, and commit operation rules? [Consistency, Spec User Story 2; Spec FR-022; Data Model PendingChange; Contract Commit Pending Changes]
- [X] CHK017 Do plan structure decisions align with the requirement that utility workflows be available without closing the GUI? [Consistency, Spec FR-009; Plan Project Structure]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK018 Are success criteria measurable for partition-only crop generation and resume behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-001..SC-002]
- [X] CHK019 Is the "no more than two user actions" crop-to-source-image outcome defined in a way that can be objectively assessed? [Measurability, Spec SC-003]
- [X] CHK020 Is the "95% of ordinary label corrections" criterion scoped clearly enough to identify which correction types count as ordinary? [Measurability, Spec SC-004]
- [X] CHK021 Are label-standardization outcomes measurable for approved labels, underscore removal, and unknown-label reporting? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-005..SC-006]
- [X] CHK022 Are operation summary outcomes measurable for every curation operation listed in the requirements? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-008; Spec FR-017]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK023 Are primary reviewer journeys covered for partition selection, crop generation, crop browsing, source-image context, relabeling, and save/commit? [Coverage, Spec User Stories 1-3]
- [X] CHK024 Are alternate flows specified for resuming existing partition crops and refreshing only changed images? [Coverage, Spec User Story 5]
- [X] CHK025 Are exception flows specified for missing images, missing annotations, corrupt annotations, unsupported shapes, and missing crop files? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases; Contract Detect Missing Crops]
- [X] CHK026 Are recovery flows specified for cancelling pending deletions and avoiding stale unsaved edits during utility operations? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases; Spec FR-010; Spec FR-022]
- [X] CHK027 Are migration scenarios specified for legacy scripts becoming wrappers and legacy crop folders being imported explicitly? [Coverage, Plan Project Structure; Quickstart Legacy Script Compatibility]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK028 Are requirements documented for source image names, crop names, and folder names that contain separators or class-like strings? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK029 Are requirements documented for ambiguous or unknown legacy labels, including reviewer decision before applying changes? [Edge Case, Spec FR-015; Data Model ClassLabelVocabulary]
- [X] CHK030 Are requirements documented for concurrent or overlapping operations such as label edits during crop generation? [Edge Case, Spec Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK031 Are requirements documented for stale manifests when annotations change after crops were generated? [Edge Case, Spec FR-004..FR-005; Data Model Partition]
- [X] CHK032 Are requirements documented for operation failure states and partially written annotation files? [Edge Case, Data Model OperationRun; Contract Commit Pending Changes]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK033 Are GUI responsiveness requirements defined for long-running crop generation and validation work? [Non-Functional, Plan Technical Context; Plan Constitution Check]
- [X] CHK034 Are scale requirements defined for the current 56,176 image/annotation dataset and 10,000-image partitions? [Non-Functional, Spec FR-020; Plan Technical Context]
- [X] CHK035 Are filesystem safety requirements defined for atomic manifest writes and annotation saves? [Non-Functional, Data Model CropManifest; Contract Commit Pending Changes]
- [X] CHK036 Are observability requirements defined through operation summaries, warnings, errors, changed files, and append-only operation logs? [Non-Functional, Spec FR-017; Data Model OperationRun]
- [X] CHK037 Are portability requirements defined for Windows-first paths while avoiding hard-coded absolute paths in refactored code? [Non-Functional, Plan Technical Context; Plan Current Architecture Findings]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK038 Are assumptions about JSON annotations as the source of truth explicitly reflected in crop and manifest requirements? [Assumption, Spec Assumptions; Data Model AnnotationFile]
- [X] CHK039 Are dependencies on Tkinter, Pillow, local filesystem storage, and pytest justified by the plan rather than hidden in implementation tasks? [Dependency, Plan Technical Context]
- [X] CHK040 Are assumptions about single-user local review documented, including any boundaries for collaboration or multi-user editing? [Assumption, Gap]
- [X] CHK041 Are boundaries documented for what remains out of scope, such as model training, cloud storage, or replacing the GUI framework? [Gap]

## Traceability & Readiness

- [X] CHK042 Are all high-priority user stories traceable to functional requirements and success criteria? [Traceability, Spec User Stories; Spec Requirements; Spec Success Criteria]
- [X] CHK043 Are data model entities traceable to at least one functional requirement or clarified workflow? [Traceability, Data Model; Spec FR-001..FR-022]
- [X] CHK044 Are operation contracts traceable to GUI-visible workflows in the specification? [Traceability, Contract Curation Operations; Spec FR-006..FR-011]
- [X] CHK045 Are current architecture findings traceable to concrete refactor decisions in the plan? [Traceability, Plan Current Architecture Findings; Plan Project Structure]
- [X] CHK046 Are remaining constitution placeholders documented as a planning risk before task generation? [Gap, Plan Constitution Check]

## Notes

- This checklist validates the writing quality of the requirements and plan. It does not verify the implementation.
- Use it before `$speckit-tasks` to tighten requirements that would otherwise become ambiguous tasks.
- Reviewed on 2026-05-06 during final T073-T081 polish. Items are marked complete based on the implemented service boundaries, fixture/integration tests, updated quickstart, migration notes, GUI smoke notes, and recorded test results.
